content: How does Malachi begin with the LORD's words?
explanation: Malachi 1:2 — Even when the people are unfaithful, the LORD declares His love. The people respond, 'How have You loved us?'

content: For what does the LORD rebuke the priests?
explanation: Malachi 1:8 — They were offering blind, lame, and sick animals to the LORD. Try offering it to your governor!
options: Offering blemished animals to the LORD (despising His name)

content: How does Malachi 2:16 express the LORD's view of divorce?
explanation: The LORD honors marriage and guards covenant. Divorce was not God's original will.
options: I hate divorce, for it covers one's garment with violence

content: Who does Malachi 3:1 prophesy will come before the Lord?
explanation: Mark 1:2 applies this verse to John the Baptist, who prepares the way for Jesus.
options: A messenger (John the Baptist) to prepare the way

content: The LORD asks the people: 'Will man ___ God? Yet you are robbing Me'
explanation: Malachi 3:8 — The people robbed God by withholding tithes and offerings.

content: What kind of book does Malachi 3:16 say the LORD has, recording those who fear Him?
explanation: The LORD remembers those who fear Him and esteem His name. They are His treasured possession.

content: Malachi 4:2 prophesies that the 'sun of righteousness' will rise with what in its wings?
explanation: The Sun of Righteousness = Christ. He brings healing to those who fear the LORD.

content: The final verses of the Old Testament prophesy who will come before the great day of the LORD?
explanation: Malachi 4:5 — Jesus confirms John the Baptist fulfills the role of Elijah (Matt 11:14).

content: The LORD comes as a refiner of silver and gold to do what?
explanation: Sanctification is not destruction but purification, like refining metal.
options: Purify the sons of Levi so they may bring offerings in righteousness

content: Malachi 2:10 asks: 'Have we not all one ___? Has not one God created us?'
explanation: All people share one Father (the Creator). Therefore we must treat one another well.

content: When the people bring the full tithe, what does the LORD promise to do?
explanation: Malachi 3:11 — The LORD protects the harvest from being destroyed when the people obey in giving.
options: Rebuke the devourer for them (protect the harvest)

content: Malachi 3:10 is the only Bible verse where the LORD challenges humans to do what?
explanation: Malachi 3:10 — 'Bring the full tithe into the storehouse... and thereby put me to the test.' The LORD promises to open the windows of heaven and pour down blessing.
options: Test the LORD by bringing the full tithe
